ddls:
CREATE TABLE "validation_rules" (
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation rule */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the validation rule */,
  "attribute" TEXT /* Attribute or property being validated (e.g., pattern, min, max) */,
  "input_types" TEXT /* Input types that support the validation rule (e.g., text, number, date) */,
  "possible_values" TEXT /* Possible values for the attribute (e.g., regular expression patterns, minimum/maximum values) */,
  "constraint_description" TEXT /* Description of the constraint enforced by the rule */,
  "violation_type" TEXT /* Type of violation that occurs when the rule is not met */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the validation rule */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the rule is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rule was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rule was last updated */,
  "custom_message" TEXT /* Custom error message for the rule */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the rule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_validation_rules_violation_type FOREIGN KEY ("violation_type") REFERENCES violations ("violation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_validation_rules_input_types FOREIGN KEY ("input_types") REFERENCES input_types ("input_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "input_types" (
  "input_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each input type */,
  "input_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the input type (e.g., text, number, date) */,
  "input_type_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the input type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the input type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the input type was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("input_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "violations" (
  "violation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each violation type */,
  "violation_name" TEXT /* Name of the violation type (e.g., patternMismatch, rangeUnderflow) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the violation type */,
  "violation_message" TEXT /* Default error message for the violation */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the violation type was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the violation type was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("violation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rule_inputs" (
  "rule_input_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rule-input type mapping */,
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to validation_rules */,
  "input_type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to input_types */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_input_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_inputs_input_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("input_type_id") REFERENCES input_types ("input_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_inputs_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_id") REFERENCES validation_rules ("rule_id")
)
